title: Lesson 32: (Katakana) "ダ, ヂ, ヅ, デ, ド"

intro_cards.title: ヂ and ヅ Are Rare
intro_cards.body:
Just like their hiragana counterparts ぢ and づ, the katakana ヂ and ヅ are rarely used in modern Japanese.

In almost all cases, ジ (ji) is used instead of ヂ, and ズ (zu) is used instead of ヅ. They sound identical.

You'll encounter ジ and ズ far more often in katakana words.
